name: Percy Faith
description: Canadian-born American bandleader, orchestrator, composer and conductor (1908-1976)
abstract: Percy Faith (April 7, 1908 – February 9, 1976) was a Canadian-American bandleader, orchestrator, composer and conductor, known for his lush arrangements of instrumental ballads and Christmas standards. He is often credited with popularizing the "easy listening" or "mood music" format.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: I was working at the time on a lot of Percy Faith’s orchestra albums. And Percy asked me one day... he said, 'I’d like to put this on my next album.' And he did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with him with the Percy Faith Orchestra in '71. We did a tour of Japan.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Maxwell
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Jimmy Maxwell) is not the speaker in the transcript segment mentioning Percy Faith; the interviewer narrates a story about working in Percy Faith's orchestra. The interviewee only responds with a general agreement ('Yeah, when you're in Rome...').

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: instrumentalists like Percy Faith and André Kostelanetz. So that’s where I simply reached into my memory... We just poured those out... on these LPs
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: Andre Kostelanetz, Percy Faith, those were typical of the upper end of things you might say... we worked for them too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Broadbent
about_quotes.evidence: I can love charts by David Rose or Percy Faith, you know it’s the orchestration and the music, whatever.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Broadbent interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 26, 2023
